# PantryPal Design System
Version: 2.0
Status: Approved Design Direction

---

# Design Philosophy

PantryPal should feel like a premium mobile app designed for real families.

The inspiration is:

- Modern Apple-quality UI
- Warm Northwoods Minnesota cabin
- Premium craftsmanship
- Clean and uncluttered
- Fast and easy to use

This should NEVER resemble an old website.

Every screen should feel like a polished App Store application.

---

# Visual Theme

Imagine walking into a beautiful handcrafted pine log cabin.

The cabin should provide warmth and atmosphere.

The interface should provide clarity.

The UI should always be more prominent than the background.

---

# Colors

## Pine Green

#183B2A

Primary headers

Navigation

Buttons

Icons

---

## Cream Paper

#F7F3EB

Cards

Panels

Menus

Backgrounds for content

---

## Warm Pine

#9B6B43

Small accent color

Borders

Icons

Labels

---

## Forest Accent

#4F7B4A

Progress bars

Pantry Score

Success indicators

---

## Warm Gold

#D8B36A

Badges

Highlights

Savings

Premium accents

---

# Typography

Large headings use an elegant serif font.

Body text uses a modern sans-serif.

Spacing should be generous.

Readable at arm's length on a phone.

---

# Background

The background should resemble the INSIDE of a premium pine log cabin.

Requirements:

- Realistic
- Warm pine color
- Softly blurred
- Low contrast
- No obvious repeating pattern
- Never distract from the cards

Cards should always be the visual focus.

---

# Cards

Cards are the primary design element.

Requirements:

- Cream color
- Rounded corners (24px)
- Soft shadow
- Plenty of white space
- Consistent spacing

Cards should feel like thick premium paper sitting on a wood table.

---

# Header

The header should contain:

Rush City Edition

PantryPal

Family Pantry Intelligence

Connected • Inventory Count

Dark pine background.

Premium appearance.

---

# Navigation

Mobile first.

Bottom navigation.

Five icons:

Home

Pantry

Scan

Grocery

More

Selected tab receives a rounded highlight.

Feels like native iOS.

---

# Home Screen Layout

Order:

1. Header

2. Dashboard metrics

- Pantry Score

- Inventory Count

- Estimated Meals

- Pantry Value

3. Pantry Health

4. Tonight's Dinner

5. Smart Shopping Recommendation

6. Pantry Value Details

Scrolling should feel natural.

No oversized empty areas.

---

# Pantry Page

Every pantry item is displayed as a premium card.

Each card includes:

Item Name

Quantity

Unit

Category

Expiration

Buttons

Use

Edit

History

Cards should be easy to tap on mobile.

---

# Smart Shopping

This is PantryPal's signature feature.

Eventually it should answer:

Is this shopping trip actually worth making?

The recommendation engine will consider:

Savings

Fuel cost

Travel distance

Travel time

Multiple stops

Vehicle MPG

Value of time

Store pricing

Inventory already at home

Instead of simply listing prices, PantryPal should recommend the smartest overall shopping plan.

Examples:

"Shop only at Walmart today."

"Adding Aldi saves another $18 for only 6 extra minutes."

"Skip Costco today."

---

# Mobile First

Every screen must look excellent on a phone before desktop.

Desktop should simply expand the mobile layout.

No page should feel like a desktop website squeezed onto a phone.

---

# Animations

Very subtle.

Cards gently lift.

Buttons press naturally.

Progress bars animate.

Page transitions are smooth.

Nothing flashy.

---

# Future Rule

Whenever design decisions are made, this document takes precedence.

If a change does not improve usability, beauty, or consistency with this design system, it should not be implemented.
